summary: At higher than approximately 57 degrees Celsius, benzoyl peroxide begins breaking down into reactive species essential for initiating polymer chain growth; thus, around or slightly exceeding room temperature up until roughly mid-sixties degree celsius ensures adequate conditions are met within practical applications like dental restorations using acrylates. To ensure efficient cross-linkage between monomer units without degradation issues prior thereto necessitates maintaining heating well past initial onset but not excessively high where potential side effects might arise due excessive reactivity leading towards premature curing phenomena instead desired gradual progression toward fully formed network structure characteristic idealized end product properties sought after clinically speaking herein considered contextually appropriate range would be somewhere near vicinity mentioned earlier i.e., ~around sixty degrees centigrade being most
